user: Write a haiku about a city waking up at dawn.
Write a 150–200 word cover letter for a junior data analyst position, addressed to a hiring manager named Priya Rao, mentioning SQL and a willingness to learn.
Write an online product description (50–80 words) for a stainless steel insulated travel mug. Persuasive but not over-the-top.
Continue this story in 100–150 words, keeping the tone consistent: 'The elevator stopped between floors, and the lights flickered twice before settling into a dim, amber glow.'
Write a four-line poem about the sea that uses an ABAB rhyme scheme.
Write a polite but firm email (under 120 words) declining a meeting invitation because of a scheduling conflict, and proposing two alternative times next week.
Write the opening paragraph (about 80 words) of a mystery novel set in a coastal town. Establish atmosphere and hint that something is wrong.
Write a limerick about a programmer who can't find a bug.
Write a 3-sentence toast for a friend's 30th birthday — warm, a little funny, not cheesy.
Rewrite this sentence to be more vivid and engaging while keeping the meaning: 'The meeting was long and we discussed many things.'
Write a bedtime story for a 5-year-old (120–160 words) about a sleepy little fox. Gentle, simple vocabulary, soothing ending.
Write a tweet (under 280 characters) announcing the launch of a free open-source note-taking app called 'Margin'. Include a clear call to action.
Write a six-line free-verse poem about the feeling of finishing a long project.
Write a 100-word review of a fictional ramen shop called 'Steam & Stone'. Mention one dish, the ambiance, and one small criticism.
Write a motivational paragraph (about 90 words) for someone restarting a fitness routine after a long break — encouraging, realistic, not preachy.
Write a short dialogue (8–12 lines) between a customer and a barista in which the customer is comically indecisive. Keep it light and natural.
For an app that helps friends split bills, write a one-sentence description of the app and three distinct tagline options.
Write an 80–100 word description of a fictional mountain village in autumn, in a warm, slightly nostalgic tone.
Write a short, encouraging note (under 60 words) to leave for a coworker who is having a hard week.
Compose a four-stanza song lyric in the order verse, chorus, verse, chorus, about coming home after a long time away. The chorus must be word-for-word identical both times.
Write a 120-word piece of flash fiction that begins and ends with the exact same sentence: 'The kettle was still warm.'
